title: Simultaneously Minimizing Storage and Bandwidth Under Exact Repair With Quantum Entanglement
authors: Lei Hu, Mohamed Nomeir, Alptug Aytekin, Sennur Ulukus
abstract: We study exact-regenerating codes for entanglement-assisted distributed storage systems. Consider an $(n,k,d,α,β_{\mathsf{q}},B)$ distributed system that stores a file of $B$ classical symbols across $n$ nodes with each node storing $α$ symbols. A data collector can recover the file by accessing any $k$ nodes. When a node fails, any $d$ surviving nodes share an entangled state, and each of them transmits a quantum system of $β_{\mathsf{q}}$ qudits to a newcomer. The newcomer then performs a measurement on the received quantum systems to generate its storage. Recent work [1] showed that, under functional repair where the regenerated content may differ from that of the failed node, there exists a unique optimal regenerating point that \emph{simultaneously minimizes both storage $α$ and repair bandwidth $d β_{\mathsf{q}}$} when $d \geq 2k-2$. In this paper, we show that, under \emph{exact repair}, where the newcomer reproduces exactly the same content as the failed node, this optimal point remains achievable. Our construction builds on the classical product-matrix framework and the Calderbank-Shor-Steane (CSS)-based stabilizer formalism.
summary: This paper shows that in quantum-assisted distributed storage, we can achieve the most efficient possible balance between storage space and repair data even when we require the repaired node to be an exact replica of the original. It achieves this by using quantum stabilizer codes to optimize the reconstruction process.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether the optimal tradeoff between storage and repair bandwidth achieved under functional repair can be maintained under the stricter constraint of exact repair in entanglement-assisted distributed storage systems.
main_result: The authors prove that for the regime d >= 2k - 2, the optimal regenerating point where storage and repair bandwidth are both minimized to B/k is achievable under exact repair.
method: The construction utilizes a classical product-matrix framework combined with the Calderbank-Shor-Steane (CSS)-based stabilizer formalism and Vandermonde matrices.
model_or_system: An entanglement-assisted distributed storage system consisting of n nodes storing a file of B classical symbols, where node failure recovery involves d helper nodes sharing a prior entangled state and transmitting qudits to a newcomer.
important_parameters: n (total nodes), k (nodes for retrieval), d (helper nodes), alpha (storage per node), beta_q (quantum repair bandwidth), and the regime d >= 2k - 2.
main_assumptions: The construction assumes noiseless quantum channels and the availability of prior entanglement.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the workflow of the entanglement-assisted system, showing the process of information encoding, transmission of qudits, and the newcomer performing stabilizer measurements for regeneration.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of exact repair in entanglement-assisted systems, presents a construction using CSS codes and product matrices, proves the achievability of the optimal regenerating point for d >= 2k - 2, and discusses scalability for larger d.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1. The entanglement-assisted distributed storage system under exact repair when (n, k, d) = (6, 3, 4), α = 2, and βq = 1 in Example 1.
